channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to use Passwords, Apple iOS 18’s new iPhone password management app
item.description: Apple iOS 18, launched in September, is one of the most important updates to the iPhone’s operating system in the device’s history. Why? Because it lays the groundwork for Apple Intelligence, Apple’s artificial intelligence platform, which will power the phone in the years ahead.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: A haunting Patch Tuesday for October: 117 updates (and 5 zero-day flaws)
item.description: This month’s Patch Tuesday delivers a large set of patches from Microsoft that fix 117 flaws, including five zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43573, CVE-2024-6197, CVE-2024-20659, CVE-2024-43572 and CVE-2024-43583).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump campaign, Musk coordinated to stop hacked material on X: Report
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign worked with Elon Musk’s X to prevent links to hacked campaign materials from circulating on the social media platform last month, according to The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Embedded developers face mounting pressure on security
item.description: Developers and software engineers face mounting pressure to balance rapid innovation with safety and security, says software provider BlackBerry Limited. The company released the results of its survey of 1,000 embedded software developers and engineers worldwide on October 8.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Nube, ‘edge’ e IA: cómo sacar el máximo partido a este trinomio tecnológico
item.description: El mundo de TI está siendo testigo de una convergencia cada vez más cercana de la nube, el edge y la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Getting the timing right at Setterwalls to invest in AI support
item.description: In recent years, there’ve been a number of smaller AI products geared toward the legal profession, but it wasn’t until gen AI caught on that Swedish law firm Setterwalls really saw the benefit. “We saw that there was potential to benefit on a broad front,” says CIO Daniel Sandberg.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: More than one-third of cloud environments are critically exposed, says Tenable
item.description: In the first half of this year, 38% of organizations had at least one cloud workload that was critically vulnerable, highly privileged, and publicly exposed, according to a study of telemetry from customers of cloud security vendor Tenable released this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Why did the 2024 Nobel for physics go to AI-related research?
item.description: The award this week of Nobel prizes in chemistry and physics to a small number of artificial intelligence pioneers affiliated with Google has stirred debate over the company’s research dominance and how breakthroughs in computer science ought to be recognised.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The inside story of Roku’s TV program
item.description: Ten years ago, streaming stick maker Roku released its first smart TVs in partnership with Hisense and TCL, two Chinese TV manufacturers that few had heard of in the U.S. at that time. In the years since, Roku has become the domestic smart TV market leader.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab,' plans to bring autonomous driving tech to other models in 2025
item.description: Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Login.gov Rolls Out Facial Recognition Option for Users - Secure Identity Verification Now Available
item.description: GSA's Login.gov online hub offers facial recognition technology for identity verification, meeting NIST standards and providing secure access to government services.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A Severe Solar Storm Headed to Earth: What You Need to Know
item.description: Solar storm headed to Earth, power grids under stress, space weather forecasters warn
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Big shift in IT employment shows new skills are needed
item.description: Multiple research firms reported that the unemployment rate for IT workers dropped precipitously last month — in one instance, by nearly 37% — a decline that marked a serious shift from months of incremental increases mixed with plateaus in unemployment.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez promises 'brawl' if 'billionaires' force out Lina Khan
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) vowed Wednesday there would be a “brawl” if wealthy Democratic donors attempt to force Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan out of a potential Harris administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft wants Copilot to be your new AI best friend
item.description: “It’s great to see you!” Microsoft’s Copilot AI chatbot underwent a transformation last week, morphing into a simplified pastel-toned experience that encourages you…to just chat.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Electron vs. Tauri: Which cross-platform framework is for you?
item.description: If there’s one thing developers agree on about cross-platform applications, it’s that it’s easier to create a GUI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript than to use one of the many cross-platform frameworks out there. Frameworks like Electron give you the best of both worlds.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WebSockets under the hood with Node.js
item.description: WebSockets is a network communication protocol that enables two-way client-server communication. WebSockets are often used for applications that require instantaneous updates, using a persistent duplex channel atop HTTP to support real-time interactions without constant connection negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with four features so far proposed for the release: a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface), and a late barrier expans
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Julia language adds lower-overhead Memory type
item.description: A new version of the dynamically typed, high-performance Julia language for numerical computing has been released. Julia 1.11 features a new Memory type, a lower-level container that provides an alternative to Array. Downloadable from julialang.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Embracing your inner on-premises self
item.description: In a significant development in the cloud computing industry, AWS, a leading hyperscaler, has recently acknowledged considerable competition from on-premises IT solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SAP Build gains AI capabilities to help build autonomous agents
item.description: SAP wants developers to view its Build platform as the one extension solution for all of SAP’s applications, according to Michael Aneling, chief product officer for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
item.dc:creator: Info World
